description: Recording simulation data over time using the history command in PFC 5.0. Covers FISH function history, wall history, measurement history, ball history, and mechanical history.

sections.heading: FISH Function History
sections.content:
Record the return value of a FISH function at each timestep.

Syntax:
  history id N @fish_function_name

  id N                  — unique history ID for later reference/plotting
  @fish_function_name   — name of a FISH function (must return a value)

Example:
  ; Define a FISH function
  define axial_stress
    local wp = wall.find(6)
    local force = wall.force.contact(wp, 3)
    axial_stress = force / cross_area
  end

  ; Record it every step
  history id 1 @axial_stress

The history records whatever value the named FISH function assigns to its own name (the return value convention in FISH).

sections.heading: Wall History
sections.content:
Record wall quantities directly without defining a FISH function.

Syntax (PFC 5.0):
  history wall <quantity> id N

Available quantities:
  xcontactforce   — total x-contact force on wall ID N
  ycontactforce   — total y-contact force on wall ID N
  zcontactforce   — total z-contact force on wall ID N
  xdisplacement   — x-displacement of wall ID N
  ydisplacement   — y-displacement of wall ID N
  zdisplacement   — z-displacement of wall ID N

Examples:
  history wall xcontactforce id 3    ; force on left wall
  history wall zcontactforce id 6    ; force on top wall
  history wall zdisplacement id 6    ; top wall displacement
  history wall ydisplacement id 1    ; front wall displacement

Internally, these map to FISH functions:
  wall xcontactforce  — wall.force.contact(wp, 1)
  wall ycontactforce  — wall.force.contact(wp, 2)
  wall zcontactforce  — wall.force.contact(wp, 3)
  wall xdisplacement  — wall.disp(wp, 1)
  wall ydisplacement  — wall.disp(wp, 2)
  wall zdisplacement  — wall.disp(wp, 3)

sections.heading: Measurement History
sections.content:
Record quantities computed by measurement spheres.

Syntax:
  history id N measure <quantity> id M

  id N        — history ID
  quantity    — measured quantity
  id M        — measurement sphere ID

Available quantities:
  stressxx    — xx component of stress tensor
  stressyy    — yy component of stress tensor
  stresszz    — zz component of stress tensor
  stressxy    — xy component of stress tensor
  stressxz    — xz component of stress tensor
  stressyz    — yz component of stress tensor
  porosity    — porosity of measurement region
  coord       — coordination number (avg contacts per particle)

Examples:
  measure create id 1 radius 0.03 x 0 y 0 z 0
  history id 10 measure stresszz id 1
  history id 11 measure porosity id 1
  history id 12 measure coord id 1

sections.heading: Ball History
sections.content:
Record properties of individual balls.

Syntax:
  ball history <quantity> id N

  quantity    — property to record
  id N        — ball ID

Available quantities:
  xvelocity   — x-velocity of the ball
  yvelocity   — y-velocity of the ball
  zvelocity   — z-velocity of the ball
  xposition   — x-position of the ball
  yposition   — y-position of the ball
  zposition   — z-position of the ball
  xforce      — resultant x-force on the ball
  yforce      — resultant y-force on the ball
  zforce      — resultant z-force on the ball
  displacement — total displacement magnitude

Example:
  ball history zvelocity id 5    ; track a specific ball's z-velocity
  ball history xposition id 5    ; track ball position

Note: Replace 'ball' with 'clump' or 'pebble' for clump histories.

sections.heading: Mechanical History
sections.content:
Record global mechanical state variables.

Syntax:
  history mechanical solve <quantity>

Available quantities:
  unbalanced  — maximum unbalanced force ratio (average ratio)
  ratio       — same as unbalanced
  step        — current step number
  age         — mechanical time (real or timestep count)

Example:
  history id 3 mechanical solve unbalanced

This records the average ratio (maximum unbalanced / average contact force), which is useful for monitoring convergence during solving.

sections.heading: History Recording Frequency
sections.content:
Control how often histories are recorded.

Syntax:
  set hist_rep N

  N   — record every N steps (default: 1 = every step)

Example:
  set hist_rep 10     ; record history every 10 steps

When 'solve' command is used, histories are recorded at solve intervals. With 'set hist_rep', you can reduce data volume during long runs without sacrificing early-step data.

To write history data to a file during execution:
  history write id N file 'filename.his'
  history write id N file 'filename.his' table <table_name>

To display history in plot:
  plot item create history 1 vs 2
  (plots history 2 on x-axis vs history 1 on y-axis)

sections.heading: Accessing History Data in FISH
sections.content:
Read history values programmatically:

  his.find(id)           — get history pointer by ID
  his.time(hip)          — get time/value at current step
  his.start(hip)         — initial time/value
  his.end(hip)           — final (current) time/value
  his.label(hip)         — history label string

Example:
  local hip = his.find(1)
  local current_val = his.end(hip)
  local label = his.label(hip)

To export history data to a table:
  history table <table_name> id N

Example:
  history table 'stress_data' id 1
  history table 'strain_data' id 2
  ; then plot or export the tables

sections.heading: Practical Notes
sections.content:
- Always assign explicit history IDs with 'id N' for clarity. If omitted, IDs are auto-assigned but harder to reference.
- History IDs must be unique. If you reuse an ID, the old history is overwritten.
- For long simulations, use 'set hist_rep 100' or higher to reduce memory/file size.
- Wall history ('history wall zcontactforce id N') is equivalent to a FISH function returning 'wall.force.contact(wall.find(N), 3)', but more concise.
- For complex derived quantities (e.g., stress ratio), define a FISH function and use 'history id N @function'.
- In PFC 5.0, measurement history automatically creates the measurement sphere if it doesn't exist for the specified ID.
- To write all histories to a file: 'history write id all file "output.his"' at the end of simulation.
- The 'history write' command writes tab-separated columns: step, value1, value2, ...
